name: Amandla Community Development Corporation
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor certified by MSHDA and HUD offering free foreclosure prevention, homebuyer education, and financial counseling in the Detroit area.
description_long:
Amandla Community Development Corporation is a Detroit-based non-profit that serves the Northwest Detroit, Warren, and Livonia metropolitan area. Founded to address housing instability, Amandla operates as a member of the Michigan State Housing Development Authority (MSHDA) Counseling Network and delivers HUD and MSHDA-certified counseling services at no cost to clients.

Amandla provides comprehensive housing and financial counseling through both individual and group sessions. Services include pre- and post-purchase homebuyer counseling, mortgage and property tax foreclosure prevention (including the Step Forward Michigan program), rental counseling, budgeting, credit counseling, and financial capability training. All counseling is delivered by MSHDA and/or HUD-certified counselors in confidential settings, with remote options available since the COVID-19 pandemic.

What distinguishes Amandla is its deep integration with government housing programs and its specific focus on foreclosure prevention through direct lender negotiation. The organization facilitates access to loss mitigation options and works directly with lenders and attorneys on behalf of homeowners in default. Their Step Forward Michigan program specifically targets homeowners facing mortgage or property tax foreclosure, with documented eligibility requirements and structured application processes.

Amandla's primary limitation is geographic—services are restricted to the Detroit, Warren, and Livonia MSA. Additionally, the website notes that in-person appointments were temporarily suspended due to COVID-19, with services moved to remote delivery, which may be a barrier for those without reliable internet access. The organization focuses exclusively on housing and financial counseling rather than broader debt relief or credit repair services.
pros: HUD and MSHDA-certified counselors providing free services with no cost to clients
Specialized foreclosure prevention through direct lender and attorney negotiation for mortgage and property tax issues
Access to Step Forward Michigan program with structured eligibility requirements and documented assistance
Comprehensive pre-purchase counseling including credit review, budgeting, and mortgage readiness assessment
Group workshops available alongside individual counseling for cost-effective education
Confidential one-on-one counseling addressing credit, budgeting, and financial capabilities
Member of MSHDA Counseling Network, indicating compliance with state housing authority standards
cons: Services geographically limited to Detroit, Warren, and Livonia MSA only—does not serve other Michigan regions or states
Website indicates in-person services remain suspended with shift to remote-only delivery, excluding those without internet access
No clear information on wait times, appointment availability, or response timeframes for service requests
Limited to housing and financial counseling—does not offer debt settlement, credit repair, or broader debt relief services
Website outdated language regarding COVID-19 suspension with no clear re-opening timeline or current service status
best_for: Detroit-area homeowners facing foreclosure seeking free negotiation assistance with lenders
First-time home buyers in the MSA preparing for mortgage qualification and purchase
Homeowners struggling with property tax foreclosure or mortgage default in Michigan
Renters seeking affordable counseling on lease terms, fair housing rights, and rental subsidies
diagnosis: Amandla is ideal for Detroit-area homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking homebuyer education from a government-certified non-profit with no fees. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to the Detroit, Warren, and Livonia MSA, plus reliance on remote-only service delivery that may exclude those without reliable internet access.
services: HUD and MSHDA-certified individual counseling sessions
Pre-purchase homebuyer counseling with credit review and mortgage readiness assessment
Post-purchase counseling on home maintenance, escrow, refinancing, and homeowner responsibilities
Mortgage foreclosure prevention with direct lender and attorney negotiation
Step Forward Michigan program administration for eligible homeowners
Rental counseling including fair housing, landlord-tenant law, and subsidy program information
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
